qa_pairs.question: What does a red x-mark signify when submitting an Alliance CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: A red x-mark indicates that content is missing in that particular row of your Alliance CCV submission.

qa_pairs.question: How do I edit information in a CCV row that has a red x-mark?
qa_pairs.answer: You should click on the pencil icon next to the row with the red x-mark to edit the information. The CCV interface will then tell you which fields are missing or filled out incorrectly.

qa_pairs.question: What button should I click to view my CCV before final submission?
qa_pairs.answer: To preview your CCV submission, you should click on the 'Preview' button.

qa_pairs.question: How do I initiate the submission of my CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: To begin the submission process for your CCV, you need to click on the 'Submit' button.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to record the confirmation number after submitting my CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: It is mandatory to make note of the confirmation number assigned upon submission because you will need to enter this number on the CCDB to link your Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application with your CCV submission.
